title: Variable Declaration
description: Declare every internal AGREE variable explicitly with eq and a type before use.
rationale: This defensive rule prevents common AGREE syntax, scope, typing, or structural-binding failures.

title: No Port Redeclaration
description: Do not redeclare AADL feature names as AGREE eq variables; ports are already visible in legal scopes.
rationale: This defensive rule prevents common AGREE syntax, scope, typing, or structural-binding failures.

title: Package Type Aliases
description: Define int, real, and bool aliases at package level when the model uses those aliases.
rationale: This defensive rule prevents common AGREE syntax, scope, typing, or structural-binding failures.

title: With Clauses
description: Add matching with clauses for package-qualified references such as Base_Types::Float.
rationale: This defensive rule prevents common AGREE syntax, scope, typing, or structural-binding failures.

title: Type Scope Placement
description: Place eq, const, assume, and guarantee clauses in component type annexes for interface behavior.
rationale: This defensive rule prevents common AGREE syntax, scope, typing, or structural-binding failures.

title: Implementation Placement
description: Place implementation-oriented assign clauses in component implementation annexes.
rationale: This defensive rule prevents common AGREE syntax, scope, typing, or structural-binding failures.

title: Assign LHS Exists
description: The left-hand side of assign must already exist as an output feature or declared auxiliary variable.
rationale: This defensive rule prevents common AGREE syntax, scope, typing, or structural-binding failures.

title: No Double Definition
description: Do not define the same variable with both an initialized eq expression and an implementation assign.
rationale: This defensive rule prevents common AGREE syntax, scope, typing, or structural-binding failures.

title: Logical Operators
description: Use AGREE logical operators and, or, and not (...); do not use &&, ||, or !.
rationale: This defensive rule prevents common AGREE syntax, scope, typing, or structural-binding failures.

title: Equality Syntax
description: Use = rather than := in AGREE expressions.
rationale: This defensive rule prevents common AGREE syntax, scope, typing, or structural-binding failures.

title: Boolean Comparisons
description: Comparison expressions return bool and must not be assigned to numeric variables.
rationale: This defensive rule prevents common AGREE syntax, scope, typing, or structural-binding failures.

title: Constants
description: Declare fixed thresholds with const.
rationale: This defensive rule prevents common AGREE syntax, scope, typing, or structural-binding failures.

title: State Recurrence
description: Define pre() recurrences with the -> operator in a type-level eq declaration.
rationale: This defensive rule prevents common AGREE syntax, scope, typing, or structural-binding failures.

title: Pre Syntax
description: Use pre(x) with parentheses.
rationale: This defensive rule prevents common AGREE syntax, scope, typing, or structural-binding failures.

title: Quoted Labels
description: Use unique quoted labels for labeled assume, guarantee, assert, and assign clauses.
rationale: This defensive rule prevents common AGREE syntax, scope, typing, or structural-binding failures.

title: ASCII Syntax
description: Avoid non-ASCII mathematical or logical symbols in generated artifacts.
rationale: This defensive rule prevents common AGREE syntax, scope, typing, or structural-binding failures.

title: Unsupported Functions
description: Do not call unsupported math-library functions unless they are explicitly provided by a referenced node or package.
rationale: This defensive rule prevents common AGREE syntax, scope, typing, or structural-binding failures.

title: Requirement Chain
description: Preserve trigger, constraint, and required response in generated contracts.
rationale: This defensive rule prevents common AGREE syntax, scope, typing, or structural-binding failures.

title: Implication Direction
description: Use one-way implication => unless the requirement explicitly states equivalence.
rationale: This defensive rule prevents common AGREE syntax, scope, typing, or structural-binding failures.

title: Grounded Identifiers
description: Ground every AGREE variable in the current AADL model or an explicit declaration.
rationale: This defensive rule prevents common AGREE syntax, scope, typing, or structural-binding failures.

title: No Unsafe Subcomponent Scope
description: Do not reference implementation-only subcomponent paths from incompatible component type scopes.
rationale: This defensive rule prevents common AGREE syntax, scope, typing, or structural-binding failures.

title: Annex Scope
description: Treat each annex agree block as scoped to its owning component declaration.
rationale: This defensive rule prevents common AGREE syntax, scope, typing, or structural-binding failures.

title: Subcomponent Binding
description: Use implementation-level assignments to bind subcomponent outputs when needed.
rationale: This defensive rule prevents common AGREE syntax, scope, typing, or structural-binding failures.

title: Conditional Expressions
description: Use nested if ... then ... else expressions for branching logic.
rationale: This defensive rule prevents common AGREE syntax, scope, typing, or structural-binding failures.

title: No Elsif
description: Do not use unsupported elsif syntax.
rationale: This defensive rule prevents common AGREE syntax, scope, typing, or structural-binding failures.

title: AGREE Scalar Type Names
description: If AGREE reports "Couldn't resolve reference to 'integer'" or "Couldn't resolve reference to 'boolean'" in a const or eq declaration, the generated AGREE type name is invalid. AGREE scalar declaration types are int, real, and bool.
bad_pattern: const lower_limit : integer = 0; eq ok : boolean = x >= lower_limit;
recommended_pattern: const lower_limit : int = 0; eq ok : bool = x >= lower_limit;
rationale: OSATE/AGREE treats integer and boolean as unresolved references in AGREE const/eq type positions.

title: Named Thing Has No Type
description: If AGREE reports "named thing must be an expression with a type" or a guarantee expression has type <error>, trace the first unresolved identifier or invalid helper declaration on the same line. Fix the root unresolved reference, or delete the invalid clause instead of adding architecture.
recommended_pattern: Use only visible AADL features or eq helpers declared in the same annex; otherwise remove the guarantee.
rationale: Many downstream type errors are cascades from one unresolved name or invalid helper type.

title: Unresolved Current-Case Identifier
description: If AGREE reports "Couldn't resolve reference to" a port, data type, package, or ModelUnit, do not invent a declaration in repair. Use exact visible identifiers from the current component scope; for package/data type failures, this is usually a model dependency staging issue rather than a contract repair issue.
bad_pattern: Adding new data types or packages inside the repaired AADL just to satisfy generated AGREE.
recommended_pattern: Delete or rewrite the AGREE clause to use existing visible identifiers; keep architecture unchanged.
rationale: Repair should not redesign the benchmark model to satisfy generated clauses.

title: Assumed Type vs Actual Type
description: If AGREE reports "The assumed type ... is ... but the actual type is ...", align the helper declaration type with the expression. Comparison expressions are bool; integer literals and integer arithmetic are int; real literals and real arithmetic are real.
bad_pattern: eq range_value : int = x >= min_value and x <= max_value;
recommended_pattern: eq range_value : bool = x >= min_value and x <= max_value;
rationale: Type mismatch repairs should change the AGREE helper type or remove the helper, not alter AADL features.

title: Implementation Scope Guarantee Error
description: If diagnostics point to implementation-level references or subcomponent paths in a component type annex, move only valid implementation bindings to the implementation annex or delete the invalid guarantee. Component type contracts may reference only features visible at that type scope.
recommended_pattern: Use a type-level guarantee over visible ports, or an implementation-level assign when the left-hand side is a valid output.
rationale: Most subcomponent scope errors are caused by copying implementation names into type-level contracts.

title: Event Port Numeric Encoding
description: If diagnostics involve an event port in a numeric or Boolean comparison, delete the clause unless the model provides an explicit data value for the event. Plain AADL event ports are not AGREE numeric values and should not be encoded as 0, 1, true, or false.
recommended_pattern: Omit the event-port activation clause or use a model-provided data/event-data value if one exists in the current scope.
rationale: Encoding event occurrence as numeric or Boolean values causes unresolved/type errors and usually invents semantics not present in the AADL model.

title: Opaque Boolean Data Range Comparison
description: If diagnostics point to range comparisons or 0/1 equality over Boolean, enumeration-like, or opaque data ports, remove the generated clause unless the requirement and AADL data representation provide a numeric scalar. Do not convert Boolean domain values into integers.
bad_pattern: assume "power_status non-negative": power_status_FSS >= 0; eq valid : bool = power_status_FSS = 0 or power_status_FSS = 1;
recommended_pattern: Use exact Boolean expressions only when the port is an AGREE bool in scope; otherwise omit the unsafe clause.
rationale: Many benchmark data ports are AADL data classifiers with Data_Model representations, and generic range constraints over them often fail AGREE type checking.

title: Invented Non-Negative Constraint
description: If the requirement does not explicitly state non-negative, positive, minimum, maximum, or a numeric interval, do not create a generic x >= 0 assumption for a port just because it looks numeric. Delete invented bounds during repair.
recommended_pattern: Keep only bounds directly stated by the requirement and compatible with a numeric AGREE type.
rationale: Generic safety bounds inflate prompts, reduce semantic fidelity, and frequently produce int/real or opaque-data type errors.

title: AADL Property Names Are Not AGREE Variables
description: If diagnostics report unresolved references to Compute_Execution_Time, Deadline, Period, Priority, Dispatch_Protocol, Stack_Size, Code_Size, or unit tokens such as ms/us/Hz/Bytes inside an AGREE annex, delete the generated clause. AADL property associations are not automatically in AGREE expression scope as variables.
bad_pattern: guarantee "timing": Compute_Execution_Time <= 3 ms and Deadline = 1000 ms;
recommended_pattern: Do not encode AADL timing properties as AGREE expressions unless the model exposes explicit typed ports or constants for them.
rationale: Scheduling properties can guide requirement interpretation, but direct property names and AADL unit literals are invalid in ordinary AGREE expressions.
